seo.title: AI Agent Authentication and Authorization in Vercel AI SDK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent authentication and authorization in Vercel AI SDK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Vercel AI SDK agents, authentication and authorization issues manifest when tool calls lack proper credential scoping, leading to unauthorized API access during streaming responses. Agents often expose sensitive operations without user context validation, especially in Server Actions and Edge Runtimes where session data isn't propagated to LLM tool invocations. This creates security gaps in multi-step agent flows using generateText and streamText, where partial auth failures go undetected amid hallucinations.

howItManifests.scenario: Unscoped database tool calls in streamText agents
howItManifests.symptom: Agent streams user data from wrong tenant during multi-turn conversation

howItManifests.scenario: Missing auth headers in external API tools
howItManifests.symptom: Tool calls fail silently with 401s, agent hallucinates responses
howItManifests.impact: Inaccurate outputs; users receive fabricated API data

howItManifests.scenario: Server Action auth bypass via direct LLM tool invocation
howItManifests.symptom: Admin-only tools accessible in user context
howItManifests.impact: Privilege escalation; full account takeover possible

howItManifests.scenario: Edge function session loss in agent loops
howItManifests.symptom: Auth tokens expire mid-conversation, tools return empty results
howItManifests.impact: Broken agent flows; poor UX and trust erosion

detection.name: Missing Auth Context in Tool Calls
detection.description: Scan generateText/streamText calls for tool definitions lacking user/session context injection

detection.name: Unauthorized API Key Exposure
detection.description: Detect hardcoded API keys or missing dynamic credential injection in tool functions

detection.name: Session Propagation Failures
detection.description: Check Server Actions and RSC for missing auth middleware in agent routes

fixes.name: Scoped Tool Auth Middleware
fixes.description: Inject user context into all tool calls using Vercel AI SDK's tool schema

fixes.name: Secure Streaming with Auth Validation
fixes.description: Validate auth at each stream chunk for long-running agent conversations

fixes.name: Edge Runtime Auth Headers
fixes.description: Propagate auth headers through Vercel Edge functions to agent tools

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Authentication and Authorization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automatically instruments Vercel AI SDK's generateText and streamText calls to detect auth failures in real-time. It wraps tool execution with auth validation middleware, catching missing user context, expired tokens, and privilege escalations before they reach the LLM. During agent runs, Vex traces session propagation across Server Actions, Edge functions, and tool calls, flagging incomplete auth chains.

Vex's remediation engine injects dynamic auth context into tool schemas and validates permissions at execution time. For streaming agents, it monitors partial responses for auth-related hallucinations and enforces structured output validation. The SDK integrates in <1 line of code and provides coverage reports showing auth compliance across your agent fleet.

Key features include automatic mock credential injection for local testing, production token rotation monitoring, and drift detection when auth patterns change across deployments.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add one line of Vex middleware to secure all your Vercel AI SDK agents instantly.
